body: ok I think the site filter just isn't in the reactive dependency graph. it only redraws when year changes because year IS observed. testing a fix tonight.
yeah that lines up with what I saw. nice catch.
looks good, one tiny nit: maybe rename the reactive to selected_site() for clarity? otherwise LGTM, approving.
good call, renamed. thanks for the quick review!
leaning toward zero-fill for display but keeping NA in the underlying data so we don't fabricate observations. will note it in the methods.
Agreed, please keep NA in the stored data. A footnote on the plot explaining the zero-fill would help reviewers.
love this idea. I can pull predicted tide height from the same source the pipeline uses. adding to v0.5.
the default palette is rough for the red/green species pair. viridis would fix it.
switched to Okabe-Ito since it's discrete and colorblind-safe. looks way better, thanks for flagging!
the download handler reads the raw df instead of the filtered reactive. easy fix, just need to point it at the right object.
Mytilus trossulus shows up as "M. trossulus", "Mytilus trossulus", and one heroic "mytulis". adding a cleaning lookup so the join stops dropping rows.
haha the mytulis one is mine probably. sorry. lookup table sounds right.
found 4 datasheets in feet, all from Oct-Nov 2025. normalize_tide_height() converts and logs which rows it touched.
This is a big improvement. Can you add a target that writes the unit-conversion log to disk so it's auditable?
done, added a log target. still marking draft until I backfill the tests.
reproduced with the Feb survey that ran 23:40-00:30. widening the prediction search to +/- 6h fixes it. adding a test.
got a cryptic error when my input had no CRS set. a clear stop() message would save people time.
yep, adding an is.na(st_crs(x)) check with a helpful message. thanks Dana!
grouping by site + date + quadrat_id and flagging n>1. surfaced two real dupes in the June data already.
soft warning only, not a hard fail, since dense barnacle quadrats can legitimately get high. just want eyes on them.
a few folks in journal club hit the aes_string deprecation. worth updating before the next session!
good catch, switching the slides to .data[[col]] tidy eval. will redo the screenshots too.
